---
title: ""
format:
  typst:
    template-partials:
      - page.typ
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{=typst}
#set text(font: "Helvetica Neue", size: 20pt, lang: "en")
#set par(leading: 0.65em, justify: false)
```

```{r setup}
#| include: false
library(sf)
library(tmap)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)

flows <- st_read(here("data/stress_flows.gpkg"), layer = "stress_flows", quiet = TRUE) |>
  mutate(score = pmax(-coalesce(flow_delta, 0), coalesce(flow_lts, 0)))
hex_centers  <- st_read(here("data/stress_flows.gpkg"), layer = "hex_centers", quiet = TRUE)
destinations <- st_read(here("data/destinations.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)
schools <- st_read(here("data/schools.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE) |>
  filter(name %in% c(
    "Jackson Street School",
    "Robert K. Finn Ryan Road Elementary School",
    "John F. Kennedy Middle School",
    "Leeds Elementary School",
    "Bridge St School",
    "Smith Vocational and Agricultural High School",
    "Northampton High School"
  ))
noho_outline <- read_sf(here("../Noho/Shapefiles/Noho_outline/Noho_outline.gpkg"))

tmap_mode("plot")
bbox <- st_bbox(hex_centers)
dir.create(here("poster_charts"), showWarnings = FALSE)

base_map <- function() {
  tm_basemap("CartoDB.Positron") +
  tm_shape(noho_outline, bbox = bbox) +
  tm_borders(col = "#aaaaaa", lwd = 1.2)
}

no_legend <- tm_layout(frame = FALSE, legend.show = FALSE)

# ── Row 1, Map A: Where People Live ───────────────────────────────────
map_pop <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(hex_centers) +
  tm_bubbles(
    col_alpha  = 0,
    fill       = "people_count",
    fill.scale = tm_scale_continuous(
      values = c("#fc4e2a", "#800026"),
      limits = NA
    ),
    fill_alpha  = 0.75,
    fill.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),
    size        = "people_count",
    size.scale  = tm_scale_continuous(values = c(5, 10),
                                      values.scale = 1,
                                      limits=NA),
    size.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),
    col         = NA
  ) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_pop, here("poster_charts/poster_pop.png"),
          width = 7.5, height = 7.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)

# ── Row 1, Map B: Getting Kids to School ──────────────────────────────
map_schools <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(schools) +
  tm_dots(fill = "#c0392b", size = 0.75) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_schools, here("poster_charts/poster_schools.png"),
          width = 7.5, height = 7.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)

# ── Row 1, Map C: Everyday Destinations ───────────────────────────────
map_dest <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(destinations) +
  tm_dots(fill = "#27ae60", size = 0.75) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_dest, here("poster_charts/poster_dest.png"),
          width = 7.5, height = 7.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)

# ── Row 2, Map A: Shortest Routes ────────────────────────────────────
map_unw <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(flows |> filter(flow_unweighted > 0)) +
  tm_lines(
    col       = "flow_unweighted",
    col.scale = tm_scale_continuous_sqrt(
      values = c("#fee090", "#f46d43", "#a50026"),
      limits = NA
    ),
    col.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),
    lwd        = "flow_unweighted",
    lwd.scale  = tm_scale_continuous(values = c(0, 10),
                                     values.scale = 4,
                                     limits = NA,
                                     trans = 'log'),
    lwd.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE)
  ) +
  # tm_shape(schools) + tm_dots(fill = "#c0392b", size = 0.3) +
  # tm_shape(destinations) + tm_dots(fill = "#27ae60", size = 0.3) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_unw, here("poster_charts/poster_unw.png"),
          width = 11.5, height = 8.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)

# ── Row 2, Map B: When Comfort Matters ───────────────────────────────
map_wtd <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(flows |> filter(flow_lts > 0)) +
  tm_lines(
    col       = "flow_lts",
    col.scale = tm_scale_continuous(
      values = c("#d1e5f0", "#4393c3", "#053061"),
      limits = NA
    ),
    col.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),
    lwd        = "flow_lts",
    lwd.scale  = tm_scale_continuous(values = c(0, 10),
                                     values.scale=4,
                                     limits = NA,
                                     trans = 'log'),
    lwd.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE)
  ) +
  # tm_shape(schools) + tm_dots(fill = "#c0392b", size = 0.18) +
  # tm_shape(destinations) + tm_dots(fill = "#27ae60", size = 0.18) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_wtd, here("poster_charts/poster_wtd.png"),
          width = 11.5, height = 8.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)

# ── Row 3, Map A: Stress You Can't Escape ─────────────────────────────
map_unav <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(flows |> filter(flow_lts >= 1600, LTS >= 3)) +
  tm_lines(
    col       = "flow_lts",
    col.scale = tm_scale_continuous(
      values = c("#f46d43", "#a50026"),
      limits = NA
    ),
    # lwd = 4,
    lwd       = "flow_lts",
    lwd.scale = tm_scale_continuous(values = c(0, 10),
                                    values.scale=4,
                                    limits = NA,
                                    trans = 'log'),
    lwd.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE)
  ) +
  # tm_shape(schools) + tm_dots(fill = "#c0392b", size = 0.18) +
  # tm_shape(destinations) + tm_dots(fill = "#27ae60", size = 0.18) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_unav, here("poster_charts/poster_unav.png"),
          width = 11.5, height = 8.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)

# ── Row 3, Map B: Streets That Matter Most ────────────────────────────
map_avoid <-
  base_map() +
  tm_shape(flows |> filter((flow_delta < 0 | flow_lts > 0), LTS >= 3, score >= 15000)) +
  tm_lines(
    col       = "score",
    col.scale = tm_scale_continuous(
      values = c("#f46d43", "#a50026"),
      limits = NA
    ),
    col.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE),
    lwd       = "score",
    lwd.scale = tm_scale_continuous(values = c(0, 10),
                                    values.scale=4,
                                    limits = NA,
                                    trans = 'log'),
    lwd.legend = tm_legend(show = FALSE)
  ) +
  # tm_shape(schools) + tm_dots(fill = "#c0392b", size = 0.18) +
  # tm_shape(destinations) + tm_dots(fill = "#27ae60", size = 0.18) +
  no_legend

tmap_save(map_avoid, here("poster_charts/poster_avoid.png"),
          width = 11.5, height = 8.0, units = "in", dpi = 150)
```

```{=typst}
// ── TITLE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#block(
  fill: rgb("1a1a2e"),
  width: 100%,
  inset: (x: 1em, y: 0.8em),
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  #grid(
    columns: (1fr, auto),
    gutter: 2em,
    align: (left + horizon, right + horizon),
    stack(
      spacing: 0.4em,
      text(fill: white, size: 52pt, weight: "black")[
        Where Stress Holds Northampton Cyclists Back
      ],
      text(fill: rgb("EEEEEE"), size: 24pt, style: "italic")[
        Not all streets feel safe to bike. We mapped the gap to show which streets, if made safer, would help the most.
      ],
    ),
    text(fill: rgb("AAAAAA"), size: 17pt)[
      Kent S Johnson\
      Northampton, MA\
      April 2026
    ],
  )
]

#v(0.28in)

// ── ROW 1: population → schools → destinations ────────────────────────
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 0.35in,
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[Where People Live],
    v(0.3em),
    image("poster_charts/poster_pop.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      Circles show where Northampton residents live, sized by population. These are the starting points for every trip in the analysis.
    ],
  ),
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[Getting Kids to School],
    v(0.3em),
    image("poster_charts/poster_schools.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      Seven public schools serve Northampton's children. Whether families can reach their schools by bike — comfortably, not just theoretically — shapes whether they ever try.
    ],
  ),
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[Everyday Destinations],
    v(0.3em),
    image("poster_charts/poster_dest.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      Beyond schools, people travel to libraries, the YMCA, grocery stores, parks, downtown shops, and medical facilities. These are places a connected cycling network should reliably reach.
    ],
  ),
)

#v(0.28in)

// ── ROW 2: route flows ────────────────────────────────────────────────
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 0.4in,
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[The Shortest Routes],
    image("poster_charts/poster_unw.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      If cyclists could ignore traffic stress and always take the shortest route, this is where bicycle traffic would concentrate. Bright red lines carry the most riders, including high-speed and high-traffic roads that many cyclists do not feel safe using.
    ],
  ),
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[When Comfort Matters],
    image("poster_charts/poster_wtd.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      Cyclists avoid high-stress streets, routing onto calmer alternatives even when longer. The network shifts. Blue lines show where cyclists feel safe. This is Northampton's current cycling backbone.
    ],
  ),
)

#v(0.28in)

// ── ROW 3: stress analysis ────────────────────────────────────────────
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 0.4in,
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[Stress You Can't Escape],
    image("poster_charts/poster_unav.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      Some high-stress segments have no good alternative. These segments have  significant bicycle traffic. Improving just a handful could unlock safer routes for thousands of trips.
    ],
  ),
  stack(
    spacing: 0.12em,
    text(size: 28pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[Streets That Matter Most],
    image("poster_charts/poster_avoid.png", width: 100%),
    v(0.08em),
    text(size: 19pt, fill: rgb("555555"))[
      Streets cyclists actively detour around and streets that remain unavoidable despite their stress. These are Northampton's most critical cycling infrastructure needs.
    ],
  ),
)

#v(0.22in)
#line(length: 100%, stroke: 1.5pt + rgb("cccccc"))
#v(0.18in)

// ── METHODOLOGY ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 0.6in,
  align: top,
  [
    #text(size: 22pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[How This Was Made]
    #v(0.35em)
    #text(size: 16pt, fill: rgb("444444"))[
      Every street in Northampton has been rated for *Level of Traffic Stress (LTS)* — a measure of how safe and comfortable it feels to bike. LTS 1 is comfortable for almost anyone; LTS 4 is suitable only for experienced riders.

      Using residential address data, we overlaid a hexagonal grid on Northampton and found the population-weighted center of each cell. For each of roughly 23,000 origin-destination pairs, we calculated two routes: the shortest route by distance, and the least-stressful route. Comparing these routes reveals which streets people avoid, which remain unavoidable, and where infrastructure investment would have the broadest impact.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #text(size: 22pt, weight: "bold", fill: rgb("1a1a2e"))[About This Analysis]
    #v(0.35em)
    #text(size: 16pt, fill: rgb("444444"))[
      This analysis supports conversations about where cycling infrastructure investment in Northampton would have the greatest effect — not just for one neighborhood, but across the full city.

      High-stress streets identified here are candidates for traffic calming, protected lanes, or signal improvements. Even modest changes on a few key segments could make cycling comfortable and practical for thousands more residents.
    ]
    #v(0.6em)
    #text(size: 13pt, fill: rgb("999999"))[
      Analysis and mapping: Kent S Johnson ·
      LTS criteria: Peter Furth, Northeastern University ·
      Data: OpenStreetMap and municipal address data ·
      Copyright 2026 Kent S Johnson #h(0.4em) #box(image("poster_charts/cc-by-nc-sa.png", height: 11pt)) #h(0.3em) #link("https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/")[CC BY-NC-SA 4.0]
    ]
  ],
)
```
